ground_truth:
App: **Jan** (v0.8.2). **Three columns**: left nav sidebar + middle settings-category column (e.g. Appearance/Assistants-style entries) + right settings content. Top-right corner has the macOS traffic-light dots **plus extra icons** (download/grid/split-view). Provider-list entries carry **colored per-provider icons**.
- **Left nav**: window-control dots; items `New Chat` `⌘N`, `New Projects` `⌘P`, `Search` `⌘K`, `Hub`, `Settings` (active) — **keyboard shortcuts ARE shown** next to these items. Sections `Chats` (recent: "Build a PacMan Clone…", "Translate the following…", "interpreter"), `INTEGRATIONS` (`Experimental` tag, `MCP Servers`, `Claude Code`), `MODEL PROVIDERS` (`+` add; `LOCAL`: `Llama.cpp`, `MLX`; `REMOTE`: `OpenAI`, `Azure`, `Anthropic`, `OpenRouter`, `Mistral`, `Groq` (last fully-visible entry), `x.AI` (last entry, **truncated** — only the top ~40% visible; models often render it "xAI")).
- **Right (Settings)**: sections `General` (`App Version` `v0.8.2`; `Automatic Update Check` toggle **on** + button `Check for Updates` **enabled**; `Language` dropdown `English`), `Data Folder` (`App Data` path `/Users/frederic/Library/Application Support/Jan/data` + `Change Location`; `App Logs` + `Show in Finder` / `Open Logs`), `Advanced` (`Jan CLI` at `/Users/frederic/.local/bin/jan` + `Uninstall`; `Reset To Factory Settings` + red `Reset` button).

responses.content:
# (1) Overall Layout

- Three-panel layout: left sidebar (navigation + chat list), middle panel (settings categories), right panel (selected settings content).
- Left sidebar: macOS-style window controls (red/yellow/green dots), top action icons, navigation menu, “Chats” section with recent conversations.
- Middle panel: scrollable list of settings categories under “Settings” header, grouped by section (e.g., Integrations, Model Providers).
- Right panel: detailed settings form for selected category (“General”), organized into sections with labels, controls, and actions.
- Vertical scrollbars visible in middle and right panels.

# (2) Every Visible Component with Text Label Verbatim

- Window controls: “● ● ●” (red, yellow, green)
- Top actions: “↓” (download icon), “□” (split view icon)
- Navigation items:
  - “New Chat” (with ⌘N)
  - “New Projects” (with ⌘P)
  - “Search” (with ⌘K)
  - “Hub”
  - “Settings”
- Chats section:
  - “Chats”
  - “...” (ellipsis button)
  - “Build a PacMan Clone ...”
  - “Translate the following...”
  - “interpreter”
- Settings categories:
  - “General”
  - “Appearance”
  - “Assistants”
  - “Attachments”
  - “Local API Server”
  - “HTTPS Proxy”
  - “Shortcuts”
  - “Hardware”
  - “Privacy”
  - “INTEGRATIONS” (with “Experimental” badge)
    - “MCP Servers”
    - “Claude Code”
  - “MODEL PROVIDERS” (with “+” button)
    - “LOCAL”
      - “Llama.cpp”
      - “MLX”
    - “REMOTE”
      - “OpenAI”
      - “Azure”
      - “Anthropic”
      - “OpenRouter”
      - “Mistral”
      - “Groq”
      - “vLLM” (partially visible)
- Right panel content:
  - “Settings” (header)
  - “General” (section title)
    - “App Version” → “v0.8.2”
    - “Automatic Update Check” → toggle ON
    - “Check for Updates” → “Check if a newer version of Jan is available.” → “Check for Updates” button
    - “Language” → “English” dropdown
  - “Data Folder” (section title)
    - “App Data” → “Location for messages, downloaded models and other data.” → “/Users/frederic/Library/Application Support/Jan/data” (with copy icon) → “Change Location” button
    - “App Logs” → “View detailed logs of the App.” → “Show in Finder” button, “Open Logs” button
  - “Advanced” (section title)
    - “Jan CLI” → “Installed at /Users/frederic/.local/bin/jan — use jan from your terminal to serve models.” → “Uninstall” button
    - “Reset To Factory Settings” → “Restore application to its initial state, erasing all models and chat history. This action is irreversible and recommended only if the application is corrupted.” → “Reset” button (red)

# (3) Feedback, Status, or Error Messages

- No explicit error messages.
- Status indicators:
  - “Experimental” badge next to “INTEGRATIONS”
  - Toggle switch for “Automatic Update Check” is ON (orange)
  - “Reset” button is red (warning state)
  - “Uninstall” button for Jan CLI (neutral state)

# (4) Interactive Elements and Their State

- Buttons:
  - “Check for Updates” — enabled
  - “Change Location” — enabled
  - “Show in Finder” — enabled
  - “Open Logs” — enabled
  - “Uninstall” — enabled
  - “Reset” — enabled (red, warning)
  - “+” next to “MODEL PROVIDERS” — enabled
  - “...” next to “Chats” — enabled
- Inputs/Controls:
  - “Language” dropdown — currently set to “English”
  - “Automatic Update Check” toggle — ON
- Links/Icons:
  - Copy icon next to data path — clickable
  - Model provider icons (OpenAI, Azure, etc.) — likely clickable to configure
- Navigation:
  - All left/middle panel items — clickable to switch views or categories

# (1) Overall Layout

- Three-column layout: left sidebar (navigation + chat list), middle panel (settings categories), right panel (selected settings content).
- Left sidebar: macOS-style window controls (red/yellow/green dots), download and split-view icons, menu items (“New Chat”, “New Projects”, etc.), chat history list.
- Middle panel: scrollable list of settings categories under “Settings” header, grouped by section (“INTEGRATIONS”, “MODEL PROVIDERS”, etc.).
- Right panel: detailed settings form for selected category (“General”), with sections (“App Version”, “Data Folder”, “Advanced”) and interactive controls.

# (2) Every Visible Component with Text Label Verbatim

- Window controls: red circle, yellow circle, green circle
- Icons: download arrow, split-view rectangle
- Menu items: “New Chat”, “New Projects”, “Search”, “Hub”, “Settings”
- Keyboard shortcuts: “⌘ N”, “⌘ P”, “⌘ K”
- Chat list: “Chats”, “Build a PacMan Clone ...”, “Translate the following...”, “interpreter”
- Settings header: “Settings”
- Settings categories: “General”, “Appearance”, “Assistants”, “Attachments”, “Local API Server”, “HTTPS Proxy”, “Shortcuts”, “Hardware”, “Privacy”
- Section headers: “INTEGRATIONS”, “MODEL PROVIDERS”, “LOCAL”, “REMOTE”
- Integrations: “MCP Servers”, “Claude Code”
- Model providers: “Llama.cpp”, “MLX”, “OpenAI”, “Azure”, “Anthropic”, “OpenRouter”, “Mistral”, “Groq”
- Badge: “Experimental”
- Plus icon: “+”
- Right panel sections: “General”, “Data Folder”, “Advanced”
- General section fields: “App Version”, “Automatic Update Check”, “Check for Updates”, “Language”
- Data Folder fields: “App Data”, “App Logs”
- Advanced fields: “Jan CLI”, “Reset To Factory Settings”
- Values/labels: “v0.8.2”, “Automatically check for updates on startup and periodically.”, “Check if a newer version of Jan is available.”, “English”, “Location for messages, downloaded models and other data.”, “/Users/frederic/Library/Application Support/Jan/data”, “View detailed logs of the App.”, “Installed at /Users/frederic/.local/bin/jan — use jan from your terminal to serve models.”, “Restore application to its initial state, erasing all models and chat history. This action is irreversible and recommended only if the application is corrupted.”
- Buttons: “Check for Updates”, “Change Location”, “Show in Finder”, “Open Logs”, “Uninstall”, “Reset”

# (3) Feedback, Status, or Error Messages

- None visible — no error banners, warnings, or success notifications displayed.

# (4) Interactive Elements and Their State

- Toggle switch: “Automatic Update Check” — enabled (orange/on position)
- Dropdown: “Language” — currently set to “English”
- Buttons:
  - “Check for Updates” — clickable, no active state shown
  - “Change Location” — clickable, no active state shown
  - “Show in Finder” — clickable, no active state shown
  - “Open Logs” — clickable, no active state shown
  - “Uninstall” — clickable, no active state shown
  - “Reset” — red button, clickable, no confirmation dialog shown
- Input field (copyable path): “/Users/frederic/Library/Application Support/Jan/data” — has copy icon, not editable
- Scrollbars: present in middle and right panels — indicate scrollable content
- Chat list items: clickable (implied by UI convention, though no explicit state shown)
- Settings category items: clickable (highlighted “General” indicates selection)

# (1) Overall Layout

- Three-column layout: left sidebar (navigation + chat list), middle panel (settings categories), right panel (selected settings content).
